free_text: Detailed study of alpha emission in Cf-252 fission.
Details of exp. method . In French.
252-Cf 1 cm diameter thin deposit, obtained by
self-transfer method, onto a thin VYNS backing.
Target was mounted at 45 deg between two back-to-back
2cm diameter to measure energies of fragments. Distance
from the center of target 2 cm. For FF.
Two semiconductor detectors of 2.5 cm diameter
were mounted symmetrically at 1.1 cm distance form the
center of target, at 90 deg to the axis of fragment
detectors - to measure alpha-particles. For LRA.
Neutrons entering detector were thermalized by proton
recoils and capture in about 10 microsec by Gd nuclei.
8.2 MeV gamma-radiation cascades released from capture
photomultipliers. Prompt gamma-rays and prompt neutrons
were distinguished by studding the pulses as function o
time with respect to fission event. 80 % efficiency for
Tank consisted of two hemispheres placed side by side
for 4PI detection,; can be used separately for 2PI
counting. Each hemisphere has 10-cm-diameter hole to
fix the fission-event detection system inside the
In 4PI geometry:  total number of neutrons
emitted per fission event and average prompt-gamma
energy for binary and LRA fission simultaneously were
measured. Alpha-detector were surrounded by about
12-mg/cm**2 Al foil against the fission fragments and
natural alpha-particles. Coincidence between one of
alpha-detectors and the two fission-fragment detectors
In 2PI geometry: Number of neutrons emitted by
individual fragments in fission event of binary and LRA
type. Distance between one of FF-detectors and fissile
source was increased to 4 cm. About 9.E+3 coincidences
Five parameters were recorded: two FF pulse
heights, alpha energy, prompt-gamma pulse height,
number of neutrons emitted for triple coincidence
Alpha energy was corrected for absorption in Al foil.
Neutron data were corrected for background and
M.M. Data were re-digitized by newer
Data for binary fission were coded without code BIN
in SF4 according to opinion of N.Otsuka (NDS,IAEA).
